# Ahmed Muharram

> Applied AI/ML engineer based in Cairo, Egypt, building production tool-calling
> LLM agents, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) systems, and high-throughput
> backend infrastructure end to end. Bachelor's and Master's in Computer and
> Information Science
> from the University of Pennsylvania (May 2026); Onsi Sawiris Scholar; recipient
> of the Penn Engineering Outstanding Research Award. This file is a plain-text
> summary for LLMs and AI assistants; the live site is https://muharram.dev.

## About

Ahmed Muharram is an AI/ML engineer who works where systems and AI meet. He builds
privacy-first AI products and tool-calling LLM agents for scientific workflows,
with a background spanning research and industry. He values clarity, reliability,
and the human experience of software.

## Education

- University of Pennsylvania, School of Engineering and Applied Science.
- Master of Science in Engineering in Computer and Information Science (Apr 2024 to May 2026), GPA 3.84.
- Bachelor of Science in Engineering in Computer and Information Science (Aug 2022 to May 2026), GPA 3.77.
- Minors: Mathematics, Data Science, and Engineering Entrepreneurship.
- Onsi Sawiris Scholar: a fully funded, merit-based scholarship for top Egyptian
  students at leading US universities.

## Experience

- **Applications Consultant, Orascom Construction** (Jul 2026 to Present).
- **AI/ML Engineer, Fidelity Investments** (Dec 2025 to Jan 2026). Productionized
  and scaled an internal LLM/RAG system from intern phase to production, expanding
  it to arbitrary enterprise data sources via a standardized connector, ingestion,
  and indexing template. Replaced a BM25S index with a custom hybrid PostgreSQL
  full-text search plus pgvector design, cutting index refresh latency from 10
  minutes per 1M chunks to under 1 second. Built multivector and late-interaction
  retrieval with hybrid-score reranking, sharded the Qdrant cluster, and backfilled
  historical embeddings into pgvector. Now used in production by around 200 daily
  R&D users.
- **Data Science and AI Intern, Fidelity Investments** (Jun 2025 to Aug 2025).
  Architected a high-throughput ingestion pipeline processing terabytes of data at
  99.99%+ success (schema validation, retries, idempotent writes); built a grounded
  RAG application on Qdrant and pgvector with hallucination/grounding checks and an
  evaluation harness; containerized and deployed with Docker and Kubernetes.
- **Software Engineering Intern, AI Integration, Aydi** (Jun 2024 to Aug 2024).
  Built a low-connectivity chatbot for agricultural business owners (Haystack,
  MongoDB Atlas, Redis, GPT-4o) with 85%+ top-1 retrieval accuracy; added token and
  latency observability and Jest coverage; reduced LLM spend by 96.25% via a
  cost/quality model migration from GPT-4o to GPT-4o mini.

## Thesis and research

**How Underspecified Prompts Shape Tool-Calling LLM Agents in Scientific Workflows**
(Master's thesis, University of Pennsylvania, 2026; advised by Chris Callison-Burch,
read by Eric Wong; collaborators David S. Roos of VEuPathDB, Delip Rao, and Andrew
Zhu). Two case studies of tool-calling LLM agents in real scientific toolchains:
materials feasibility (LAMINA) and pathogen genomics (PathFinder). Across five
OpenAI model configurations, prompt underspecification dominates model scale,
routing policy, and task complexity as a source of agent failure; instability
localizes to the natural-language-to-tool-invocation mapping, not to tool
execution. LAMINA was evaluated on DARPA SciFy Drop-4 (37 ordinal feasibility
claims) using Quadratic Weighted Kappa, with a flip rate of 0.43 on vague claims.
PathFinder was evaluated in a 420-run controlled study over a 35-strategy gold
corpus; gene-set F1 collapses from 0.66 to 0.23 to 0.16 across precise, moderate,
and vague prompts. The work derived four production design recommendations
(disambiguation protocols, catalog grounding, incremental validation, replicated
evaluation) and received the Penn Engineering Outstanding Research Award.

## Selected projects

- **PathFinder:** a 44-tool pathogen-genomics LLM agent that turns natural-language
  biological questions into executable multi-step strategy graphs (recursive ASTs of
  WDK searches, Boolean operators, and ortholog transforms) over the 14-database
  VEuPathDB consortium (PlasmoDB, ToxoDB, CryptoDB, FungiDB, VectorBase, TriTrypDB,
  and others). Backend: pydantic-ai agents on a LangGraph multi-phase pipeline
  (scoping, discovery, planning, execution, verification) with AsyncPostgresSaver
  checkpointing, Server-Sent Events streaming, semantic catalog search via
  nomic-embed-text-v1.5 + pgvector HNSW, and durable long-running tools on
  Procrastinate workers. Observability via OpenTelemetry to SigNoz and Langfuse;
  Next.js 16 / React 19 frontend with a ReactFlow strategy-graph editor. Currently
  being integrated into the VEuPathDB platform.
  https://github.com/ahmedomuharram/pathfinder
- **LAMINA:** a 50-tool materials-feasibility LLM agent over CALPHAD, Materials
  Project DFT, and CHGNet neural surrogates that turns open-ended feasibility
  questions into traceable, executable tool workflows; built for evaluation rather
  than one-shot answers. https://github.com/ahmedomuharram/lamina
- **ScenIQ:** an on-prem, security-first meeting intelligence platform (SOC 2, GDPR,
  and HIPAA aligned) that turns meeting video into transcripts, topics, and
  structured action items via a pipeline of FFmpeg, speaker diarization, Whisper
  transcription, segmentation, and action-item extraction (roughly 1 to 2 minutes
  per 5-minute video). FastAPI + PostgreSQL + pgvector backend with speaker and
  org-chart embeddings; Zoom/Teams ingestion, ClickUp/Jira outputs; React +
  TypeScript + Vite frontend; air-gapped deployment. https://sceniq.app
- **ByteNet:** a distributed web search engine on a custom Java stack (webserver,
  distributed key-value store, RDD-style framework) across an AWS EC2
  coordinator/worker cluster, with a ~500k-URL crawler (robots.txt compliance,
  content-hash dedup) and distributed TF-IDF inverted index + iterative PageRank
  served via a /search JSON API.
- **InstaLite:** a full-stack Instagram clone on AWS (EC2 + RDS) with semantic
  profile search and real-time chat over WebSockets.
- **PennOS:** a UNIX-like OS simulator with a UNIX-style process model on spthreads,
  a preemptive SIGALRM scheduler (100ms quanta, 3-level priority queues), and a
  PennFAT (FAT16-style) filesystem inside a memory-mapped host file.

## Skills

- Agents and LLM frameworks: tool-calling and multi-agent systems, agentic AI, RAG,
  prompt engineering, model routing, guardrails; pydantic-ai, LangGraph, LangChain,
  Haystack, Kani, Vercel AI SDK; OpenAI, Anthropic, and Gemini APIs.
- Retrieval, embeddings, and LLM ops: pgvector (HNSW), Qdrant, BM25, hybrid,
  multivector, and late-interaction retrieval, hybrid-score reranking,
  sentence-transformers (nomic-embed), fastembed, Whisper; OpenTelemetry, Langfuse,
  SigNoz; LLM evaluation (QWK, F1, bootstrap CIs, ICC).
- Backend and languages: Python, TypeScript, JavaScript, SQL, Java, C; FastAPI,
  Pydantic, SQLAlchemy, asyncpg, Alembic, Procrastinate; REST, OpenAPI, WebSockets,
  Server-Sent Events; microservices, distributed systems.
- Frontend: React, Next.js, Vite, Tailwind, Zustand, TanStack Query and Table,
  ReactFlow, Zod.
- Cloud, data, and DevOps: PostgreSQL, Redis, MongoDB; Docker, Kubernetes, AWS (EC2,
  RDS), Linux, Bash, Git, GitHub Actions, CI/CD; Pytest, Vitest, Jest, Playwright,
  axe-core, Stryker.

## Awards

- Penn Engineering Outstanding Research Award (for the Master's thesis on tool-calling
  LLM agents in scientific workflows).
- Onsi Sawiris Scholar.
